center.name: 考卡河
center.summary: 考卡河是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联19种鸟类，包括Cauca guan、Chestnut-winged chachalaca、Blackish rail等19种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The Antioquia bristle tyrant primarily is found in the lower valleys of the Magdalena and Cauca rivers in central Antioquia, northern Caldas, western Boyacá, and northern Cundinamarca departments

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: They are the Cauca River valley from Antioquia Department south to Cauca Department, the upper Dagua River valley in Valle del Cauca Department, the upper Patía River valley in Nariño Department, and the Magdalena River valley from Santander and Boyacá departments south to Huila Department. The species primarily inhabits dry to arid valleys with scrubby vegetation

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The nominate subspecies is found in Colombia's Cauca and Magdalena river valleys and on the east slope of the Andes from Nariño Department in Colombia's southwest south through eastern Ecuador into northern Peru to (or possibly just beyond) the Marañón River

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: fasciatoventris is found in northwestern and central Colombia east to the foothills of the Sierra Nevada de Santa Marta and south into the Cauca and Magdalena valleys

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: alarum is found in Colombia, from the valleys of the Sinú, Cauca, and Magdalena rivers east into Santander Department. The black-striped woodcreeper mostly inhabits humid lowland forest

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: caucae is found in central Colombia (it is named for the Cauca River)

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: Historically it was found in the Cauca River valley between Quindío Department and Cerro Munchique in Cauca, and on the Pacific slope of the Cordillera Occidental (Western Andes) in Valle del Cauca and Cauca

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The chestnut-winged chachalaca is found in northern Colombia in an area roughly bounded on the west by the Sinú River, in the east by the foothills of the Sierra Nevada de Santa Marta, and in the south by the lower Cauca and Magdalena River valleys

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The northern population is found in Colombia's Central Andes east of the Cauca River and has a small subpopulation further east past the Magdalena River Valley in the Iguaque Massif

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: omissa is found in Colombia at the northern end of the Andes, in the valleys of the Sinú, Cauca, and Magdalena rivers, and south on the Pacific slope of the Andes to Cauca Department.{{cite book | last1 =McMullan | first1 =Miles | last2 =Donegan | first2 =Thomas M

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The nominate is found in western Colombia from the middle and upper reaches of the Rio Cauca south to the upper Rio Patía

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The indigo-capped hummingbird is found in north and central Colombia, principally in Norte de Santander Department, the Magdalena River valley, and the upper Cauca River valley

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: brunnescens is found in north-central Colombia from the lower Cauca River valley east into the middle Magdalena River valley. The russet-crowned crake mostly inhabits terrestrial landscapes; in contrast to many other species in its family it is usually not found in marshes

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: caucae: the Cauca River valley in central Colombia The slaty spinetail inhabits a variety of landscapes, most of which have dense undergrowth

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: | pages = }} *Extreme eastern Panama and slightly into northwestern Colombia *In the Sierra Nevada de Santa Marta in northern Colombia *In the Serranía del Perijá on the Colombia-Venezuela border *From extreme southwestern Colombia south through western Ecuador to Loja Province *From southern Napo Province in Ecuador south to central Peru *The valleys of the Cauca and Magdalena rivers in Colombia and into western and northern Venezuela *From eastern Venezuela east across Guyana and Suriname into French Guiana *Locally in southeastern Venezuela and northern Amazonian Brazil The sooty-headed tyrannulet primarily inhabits humid evergreen forest in the tropical zone

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: pallidior: Colombia in the valleys of the upper Sinú, lower Cauca, and middle Magdalena rivers The streak-chested antpitta inhabits lowland evergreen forest in the tropical and lower subtropical zones

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The subspecies of tawny-faced gnatwren are distributed thus: *Microbates cinereiventris semitorquatus, the Caribbean slope from southeastern Nicaragua through Costa Rica and Panama into extreme northwestern Colombia, and also the Pacific slope of central Panama *Microbates cinereiventris albapiculus, the Cauca Valley of northern Colombia *Microbates cinereiventris magdalenae, the Magdalena River Valley of northern Colombia *Microbates cinereiventris cinereiventris, from Panama's Darién Province south along the Pacific coast through Colombia to southwestern Ecuador. *Microbates cinereiventris unicus, central Colombia *Microbates cinereiventris hormotus , southern Colombia, eastern Ecuador, and northeastern Peru *Microbates cinereiventris peruvianus sensu stricto, eastern Peru and western Bolivia The tawny-faced gnatwren generally inhabits the lower strata of wet, humid, primary and secondary forest, mostly below elevation

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: saucerottei is found in western and north-central Colombia on the western slope of the Western Andes and in the Cauca River valley

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The nominate subspecies of tawny-bellied hermit is found in the Western Andes of Colombia (including the valleys of the Cauca and Patía rivers) and locally south on the western Andean slope through Ecuador to western Loja Province
